title: Sapium biglandulosum
taxonomy: Sapium biglandulosum is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. This family includes over 280 genera and more than 7,000 species. Sapium biglandulosum, also known as the stinging spurge, is native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. As a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, Sapium biglandulosum has distinctive characteristics such as milky sap and simple or alternate leaves. The classification of Sapium biglandulosum at the genus level is also within the Sapindaceae family's subfamily Sapindoideae but it is more commonly associated with the family Euphorbiaceae.

intro: Sapium biglandulosum, also known as the Horse's Eye or Horse's Eye Tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities, which can aid in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Sapium biglandulosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
